definitions.definition: The process by which a changing magnetic field in one coil induces an electromotive force (emf) in another coil, even when the two coils are not touching.
definitions.example: An example of electromagnetic induction is the generation of electricity in a power plant using a large generator.

definitions.definition: The amount of magnetic field that passes through a given area, measured in webers.
definitions.example: The magnetic flux through a coil depends on the strength of the magnetic field and the area of the coil.

definitions.definition: The law that states the induced emf in a coil is equal to the rate of change of magnetic flux through the coil.
definitions.example: Faraday's law is used to design electrical generators and motors.

definitions.definition: The law that states the direction of the induced current is such that it opposes the change in the magnetic flux that induced it.
definitions.example: Lenz's law is used to determine the direction of the induced current in a coil.

definitions.definition: The energy per unit charge that drives electric current through a conductor, measured in volts.
definitions.example: The emf of a battery is the energy it provides to drive electric current through a circuit.

definitions.definition: The electric current that flows in a conductor when a changing magnetic field induces an emf in it.
definitions.example: The induced current in a coil can be used to power a device.

definitions.definition: A coil of wire that is exposed to a changing magnetic field, causing an emf to be induced in it.
definitions.example: An electromagnetic induction coil is used in a generator to produce electricity.

definitions.definition: A region around a magnet or current-carrying wire where magnetic forces can be detected.
definitions.example: The magnetic field of a bar magnet can be visualized using iron filings.

definitions.definition: The process by which a changing current in a coil induces an emf in the same coil.
definitions.example: Self-induction is used in inductors to filter out unwanted frequencies.

definitions.definition: The process by which a changing current in one coil induces an emf in another coil.
definitions.example: Mutual induction is used in transformers to increase or decrease voltage.

definitions.definition: An electrical device that uses mutual induction to transfer energy from one coil to another.
definitions.example: A transformer is used in power transmission to increase or decrease voltage.

definitions.definition: A coil of wire that opposes changes in current, used to filter out unwanted frequencies.
definitions.example: An inductor is used in electronic circuits to smooth out voltage fluctuations.

definitions.definition: The ability of a material to support the formation of a magnetic field.
definitions.example: Air has low permeability, while iron has high permeability.

definitions.definition: The ratio of the magnetic flux density to the magnetic field strength.
definitions.example: The magnetic permeability of a material depends on its composition and temperature.

definitions.definition: The amount of magnetic field that passes through a given area, measured in teslas.
definitions.example: The magnetic flux density through a coil depends on the strength of the magnetic field and the area of the coil.
